raw_text: Paper §3.2 explicitly allows "CKA, MSE, or other similarity measures" for D^l. CKA is the metric used by Zhang et al. (2025), cited in GRAPE §3.2 as the reference for intra-layer redundancy assessment.
context: Justification for using CKA as similarity measure in GRAPE

raw_text: Phase D: GRAPE Algorithm 1 (entropy-aware greedy merge with restart)
Compute per-layer redundancy R^l = Σ_{i≠j} D^l_{ij} (Eq. 11, sum form). Set entropy threshold Ê = E_0 × (1 − γ) (Eq. 10)
context: GRAPE Eq.11 redundancy and Eq.10 entropy threshold

raw_text: R^l = Σ_{i≠j} D^l_{ij} (Eq. 11 — sum, not mean)
R̃^l = (R^l − min R) / (max R − min R) (Eq. 3 — for logging only)
Ê = E_0 × (1 − γ) (Eq. 10 — entropy threshold)
The paper's pseudocode line 12 (R^l ← R^l − 2·D[i*,j*]) is consistent with the sum-over-i≠j form of R^l
context: Correctness note discussing GRAPE pseudocode line 12 vs implementation D4 deviation
